# socialverse/external — reference-driven R-package reconstructions

Pure-Python reconstructions of the R / Stata / SPSS packages that socialverse's
statistics functions replicate, each **parity-gated against the R reference**
under the [Omicverse-RebuildR](https://github.com/omicverse/omicverse-rebuildr)
protocol (the R source is the executable spec; a pytest gate asserts element-wise
numerical parity on a canonical fixture; the threshold is committed before the
port is written and never widened).

Every port here ships with `tests/r_reference_driver.R` (runs the R package on a
fixed fixture → JSON), the port module, and `tests/test_parity.py` (the gate).
socialverse's `tl/pp/pl` modules import from here instead of carrying their own
ad-hoc numerics, so the analysis backend is provably R-faithful.

## Discovery — R/Stata/SPSS packages referenced by socialverse

No runtime `rpy2`/`Rscript` calls exist; socialverse *re-implements* these in
pure numpy/scipy. The reconstruction replaces those re-implementations with
parity-verified ports. Ranked by reference count (Phase 0.5 Discovery):

| R package | socialverse module(s) | port | status |
|---|---|---|---|
| **metafor / meta** | tl/_meta*.py | `pymetafor` | ✅ rma parity-gated (8/8) + wired into `meta_random` |
| **survey** | tl/_survey | `pysurvey` | ✅ svymean/total/glm parity-gated (5/5) + wired into `survey_estimate` (coef Δ0) |
| **survival** | tl/_longitudinal | `pysurvival` | ✅ KM + Cox PH (Efron/Breslow) parity-gated (5/5) + wired into `survival` (coef Δ1e-16) |
| **did** | tl/_causal, _fect | `pydid` | ✅ Callaway-Sant'Anna att_gt/aggte parity-gated (4/4); point estimates 1e-6 (bootstrap SE documented stochastic) |
| **ergm** | tl/_network2 | `pyergm` | ✅ dyad-independent MPLE parity-gated (3/3); MCMC-MLE/SAOM documented stochastic |
| **QCA** | tl/_setmethods | `pyqca` | ✅ truth table + Quine-McCluskey + fit params parity-gated (6/6) |
| **lavaan** | tl/_psychometrics | `pylavaan` | ✅ ML CFA + fit indices parity-gated (4/4) |
| **netmeta** | tl/_meta_nma | `pynetmeta` | ✅ graph-theoretical NMA parity-gated (8/8) |
| **psych** | tl/_efa, _reliability | `pypsych` | ✅ alpha/omega/fa parity-gated (5/5) |
| **fixest** | tl/_econ | `pyfixest` | ✅ FE-OLS + clustered SE parity-gated (3/3) |
| **demography** | tl/_demography | `pydemography` | ✅ life table + Kitagawa/Oaxaca parity-gated (6/6) |
| **robumeta** | tl/_meta_rve | `pyrobumeta` | ✅ RVE (CR2, Satterthwaite) parity-gated (2/2) |
| **mada** | tl/_meta_dta | `pymada` | ✅ Reitsma bivariate DTA meta parity-gated (5/5) |
| **MatchIt** | tl/_matching | `pymatchit` | ✅ PS logit + NN matching + balance parity-gated (6/6) |

Discovery detail (33 packages, per-module counts) is regenerated by
`tests/_discovery.py`.

## Parity philosophy (from the protocol)

- **Class-1 deterministic-numerical** gate at `max_abs_err < 1e-6` (protocol hard
  ceiling) for well-conditioned outputs.
- Where the **R reference itself carries a convergence tolerance** (e.g. metafor's
  Fisher-scoring `threshold=1e-5`), the achievable parity for tolerance-dependent
  quantities is bounded by *that* tolerance, not by our port — this is documented
  per-port in `RECONSTRUCTION_REPORT.md §Known limitations`, never hidden by
  silently widening the gate.
- **Never widen the gate to make a wrong number pass.** A dip is only acceptable
  when it traces to the reference's own documented tolerance or genuine
  cross-implementation differences (e.g. mgcv≠pygam), spelled out in the report.

## Running a port's gate

```bash
cd socialverse/socialverse/external
Rscript <pkg>/tests/r_reference_driver.R      # regenerate the R reference fixture
python -m pytest <pkg>/tests/test_parity.py -q # assert parity
```
